title: Molten Vietnamese Dutch Stroop Pudding
description: A unique dessert that combines the richness of Dutch stroop with the yeasty essence of Vietnamese cuisine.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). In a medium bowl, whisk together egg yolks and sugar until pale and thick.
In a separate bowl, whisk together flour, kosher salt, and melted butter until well combined.
In a large bowl, whisk together egg, warm milk, and Dutch stroop until smooth.
Gradually add flour mixture to egg mixture and whisk until just combined.
Pour batter into 4 (6-ounce) ramekins or small cups and place in a large baking dish.
Add enough hot water to the baking dish to come halfway up the sides of the ramekins.
Bake for 15-20 minutes or until puffed and golden brown.
Remove from oven and let cool for 1-2 minutes.
Run a knife around the edges of each ramekin and invert onto plates.
Serve immediately, with caramelized sugar on top.
Return the ramekins to the oven and bake for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until the sugar is caramelized and bubbly.
